questions.text: A system which can exchange both matter and energy with the surroundings is:
questions.explanation: Example: Boiling water in an open beaker.

questions.text: Which of the following is an intensive property?
questions.explanation: Intensive properties do not depend on the amount of matter.

questions.text: The First Law of Thermodynamics is expressed mathematically as:
For an adiabatic process, which of the following is true?
questions.explanation: No heat enters or leaves the system.

questions.text: The enthalpy of all elements in their standard state is:
questions.explanation: By convention, standard enthalpy of formation of pure elements is zero.

questions.text: In an exothermic reaction, the sign of ΔH is:
questions.explanation: Heat is released to the surroundings.

questions.text: Hess's Law states that the total enthalpy change of a reaction is:
questions.explanation: It depends only on initial and final states.

questions.text: Entropy is a measure of:
A reaction is spontaneous at all temperatures if:
questions.explanation: In ΔG = ΔH - TΔS, ΔG will always be negative.

questions.text: The Third Law of Thermodynamics states that the entropy of a perfectly crystalline substance at absolute zero is:
questions.explanation: At 0 K, all molecular motion stops in a perfect crystal.

questions.text: Work done in an isolated system is:
questions.explanation: Isolated systems do not interact with surroundings.

questions.text: Cp - Cv for 1 mole of an ideal gas is:
Which is a state function?
questions.explanation: Values depend only on the state, not how it was reached.

questions.text: Unit of Entropy is:

Internal energy change at constant volume is equal to:
questions.explanation: ΔU = q + w; at constant V, w=0.

questions.text: Enthalpy change at constant pressure is equal to:
Bomb calorimeter is used to measure:
questions.explanation: It measures heat at constant volume.

questions.text: Specific heat is the heat needed to raise the temp of _____ by 1°C.
Bond dissociation energy is always:
questions.explanation: Energy must be supplied to break a bond.

questions.text: Which of the following is an extensive property?
questions.explanation: Volume depends on the amount of substance.

questions.explanation: Combustion is always an exothermic process.

questions.text: Lattice enthalpy depends on:
Isothermal expansion of ideal gas into vacuum results in work done equal to:
Entropy of vaporisation is _____ than entropy of fusion.
questions.explanation: Gases have much more disorder than liquids.

questions.text: Gibbs Helmholtz equation is:
questions.explanation: Relates enthalpy, entropy and free energy.

questions.text: A coffee cup calorimeter measures heat at constant:
Standard conditions in thermodynamics mean:
Heat of formation of Diamond is _____ while Graphite is zero.
questions.explanation: Graphite is the most stable allotrope.

questions.text: If ΔH is -ve and ΔS is -ve, reaction is spontaneous at:
questions.explanation: Lower T makes -TΔS term smaller than negative ΔH.
